_comment: CPython APIs (functions and macros) that CANNOT set a Python exception under any circumstances.
Calling these in an error-handling block cannot clobber the pending exception.
This is a broader concept than non_erroring_int_apis below: it includes refcount macros,
type-check macros, and exception-inspection APIs that are not int-returning.
Entries here should be minimal; use non_erroring_int_apis below for the detailed metadata
entries that are also consulted by error-path-analyzer agent guidance.

PyErr_Clear: Deliberately excluded from this list: PyErr_Clear IS what the
pyerr-clear-auditor check is for. Finding PyErr_Clear inside an error
block is a legitimate question ('is this clear guarded?') handled by
would just make the two scripts diverge. Let the clobber check surface
it; the pyerr-clear-auditor then reviews whether it's guarded.

_comment: Int-returning CPython APIs whose header explicitly documents that they do not raise exceptions.
Their return value is NOT an error indicator: -1 is a comparison result or flag, not an error.
Do NOT flag unchecked use of these APIs as 'missing error check'.
Do NOT flag calls to these APIs in error-handling blocks as 'exception clobbering'.
When adding to this list, cite the header file and line where 'does not raise exceptions' is documented.
A subset of no_exception_apis above, kept separate for the error-path-analyzer agent's int-return guidance.

PyUnicode_CompareWithASCIIString.docstring: This function does not raise exceptions.
PyUnicode_CompareWithASCIIString.return_semantics: strcmp-style: -1 (uni < str), 0 (equal), 1 (uni > str). Never an error.
PyUnicode_CompareWithASCIIString.confirmed_via: wrapt v2 re-audit 2026-04-12 (reports/wrapt_report_v2.md findings #19, #33 reclassified as false positives)

PyUnicode_Tailmatch.docstring: Returns 1/0 on match/no-match. Returns -1 on error (but only if the substring is invalid UTF-8, which is unusual).
PyUnicode_Tailmatch.return_semantics: 1 (match), 0 (no match), -1 (invalid input only). Treat -1 as a rare error but not a runtime allocation failure.
PyUnicode_Tailmatch.note: This is NOT the same as PyUnicode_CompareWithASCIIString — Tailmatch CAN return -1 as an error. Kept here for reference; flag its usage only when an unchecked -1 could actually propagate.

Py_IS_TYPE.docstring: Macro that wraps Py_TYPE comparison. Never fails.

PyObject_TypeCheck.return_semantics: 1 or 0. The embedded PyType_IsSubtype call cannot raise because it only walks the MRO.
PyObject_TypeCheck.note: Technically can recurse into PyType_IsSubtype which is infallible for heap types with a tp_mro set at type-creation time.

PyIndex_Check.return_semantics: 1 if the object supports __index__, 0 otherwise. No error path.

PyCallable_Check.return_semantics: 1 if the object is callable, 0 otherwise. No error path.

PyNumber_Check.return_semantics: 1 if the object supports the number protocol, 0 otherwise. No error path.
